text: Highlight Clip CZ: All of Us Sould Have Thousands of AI Agents On May 12, 2026, Binance founder CZ stated on the PBD Podcast that currency won't just be for humans anymore—AI agents are coming, and everyone should have thousands working for them. Consequently, he noted that future user metrics will likely need to combine both humans and AI agents. CZ also shared that he heard Binance currently pays around $10 million per month in AI fees. While he has encouraged the team to deploy AI further into risk monitoring, risk control, and compliance, he believes current AI models still lack the sufficient training and data required for these specific sectors. — link

text: SEC Ends Zcash Foundation Probe With No Action The Zcash Foundation said in its Q1 2026 report that the U.S. SEC has closed its investigation and does not recommend enforcement action. It noted that governance disputes at Electric Coin Company led most of its development team to depart during the quarter, but the Zcash network continued producing blocks and settling transactions normally, with no impact to user funds or privacy. As of March 31, it reported about $36.7 million in liquid assets (including 85,412 ZEC, 41.8 BTC, ~506,600 USDC, and a small amount of ETH), with average monthly operating expenses of about $272,500. — link

text: MicroStrategy for Robotics - How does RoboStrategy's capital markets model work? Raising public capital via share issuance at premiums to NAV and redeploying it into leading private robotics companies to drive per-share value growth Issuing shares above NAV is accretive: new capital exceeds the added claim on assets, boosting existing holders' NAV per share despite dilution, with a 3x premium example lifting NAV/share 18.2% and repeated cycles compounding to 131% growth Parallels to MSTR's 55x BTC-per-share growth (97% from accretive issuance) and Metaplanet's rapid NAV/share expansion illustrate the strategy

text: Wintermute: Markets Flip From "Rate Cuts" to "Rate Hikes" as Bitcoin Rally Fades Market maker Wintermute said inflation pressure is re-entering the core narrative, with positioning shifting within five trading days from "when cuts" to "whether hikes." In crypto, BTC briefly broke above $82,000 on momentum tied to Clarity Act developments before sliding back to around $78,000, down 5.7% on the week; ETH fell 10.2%, with ETH/BTC dropping to roughly 0.0275. Wintermute said last week's BTC breakout was driven more by leverage and short-covering than spot-led confirmation, and flagged $76,000–$78,000 as key near-term support, warning that higher yields and re-accelerating inflation could make an immediate institutional bid harder to sustain. — link

text: Boston Consulting Group (BCG) on stablecoin payments Of $62T in annual on-chain stablecoin volume, only $4.2T is economically meaningful after stripping out bots, protocol mechanics, and intermediary routing Real-economy payments (goods/services) are just $350-550B, or ~7% of total - the rest is trading, treasury positioning, and exchange plumbing B2B dominates real-economy flows (~40%, +65% YoY), followed by C2C remittances (~25%, +75%) and C2B (~25%); B2C is the laggard at ~10%. TRON still owns 60-80% of payment settlement via cheap USDT transfers, but its share dropped from 74% to 60% across 2025 as institutional volume migrates to BSC, Ethereum, Solana, and Polygon

text: Japan FSA Clears Path for Foreign Trust-Backed Stablecoins, Effective June 1 Japan's Financial Services Agency (FSA), the country's top financial regulator, has promulgated amendments to Cabinet Office ordinances that formally recognize certain foreign-issued, trust-structured stablecoins as "electronic payment instruments," taking effect on June 1, 2026. The changes clarify that foreign trust beneficiary rights can be treated as electronic payment instruments in Japan when the issuing jurisdiction's rules are deemed equivalent. — link

text: Crypto "Wrench Attacks" Push Exchanges and Executives to Spend Big on Security Bloomberg reported that rising kidnappings, home invasions and "wrench attacks" in crypto are driving exchanges and high-profile figures to tighten personal security. Coinbase's latest proxy filing shows the company spent about $8.7 million in 2025 on CEO Brian Armstrong's security and related protection measures, up from about $6.2 million in 2024. text: BSC Post-Quantum Upgrade Passes Test, but TPS Falls 40% BNB Chain released its BSC Post-Quantum Cryptography Migration Report, saying BSC has tested a post-quantum upgrade using ML-DSA-44 for transaction signatures and pqSTARK for consensus vote aggregation. The design remains compatible with existing addresses, RPCs, SDKs and wallets, but testing showed major data overhead: signature size rose from 65 bytes to 2,420 bytes, and cross-region TPS fell by about 40%. BNB Chain said network and data-layer scaling remain the main challenges before production deployment. — link

text: Echo Protocol Regains Key Control, Burns Attacker's Remaining 955 eBTC Echo Protocol said an admin key leak led to unauthorized eBTC minting on Monad, causing about $816,000 in losses. The team said it has regained control of the admin key and burned the remaining 955 eBTC held by the attacker. Echo said the incident currently appears limited to Monad, with no evidence that Aptos was compromised; Aptos exposure is estimated at about $71,000. The team has paused Monad bridge functions, upgraded related contracts, and warned users to beware of fake refund, claim and recovery phishing links. — link

text: Ethereum Staking Ratio Climbs to 31% Despite ETH's 26% YTD Drop Although ETH has fallen 26% YTD, its staking ratio has climbed from 29% to 31%. This steady growth reflects strong long-term holder confidence and actively reduces the circulating supply. Looking ahead, the expansion of spot ETH ETFs and on-chain tokenization could drive institutional inflows into the staking ecosystem. However, the ultimate impact on ETH price will depend on actual institutional capital allocation. — link
